title: Hobbies & Leisure
shortSummary: Talk about what you like to do, your free time, and making plans with friends.
culturalNote: Free time (o tempo livre) in Brazil is deeply social and often outdoors. Football (o futebol) is a national passion, the beach (a praia) is a way of life on the coast, and music and dance, from samba to forró, bring people together. To say what you like, Portuguese uses gostar de: gosto de música (I like music). The verb always needs de. Making plans is casual and warm, and an invitation like vamos? (shall we go?) is common. Brazilians value good company, so leisure is usually about being together.

keyPhrases.native: O que você gosta de fazer?
keyPhrases.english: What do you like to do?
keyPhrases.pronunciation: oo kee voh-SEH GOS-tah jee fah-ZEHR

keyPhrases.english: Shall we go to the cinema?

grammar.explanation: Gostar always needs de before what you like, whether a noun or a verb: gosto de café (I like coffee), gosto de viajar (I like to travel). The de contracts with articles: gosto do cinema.
grammar.commonMistake: Dropping de. It is gosto de música, never gosto música.

grammar.explanation: Portuguese uses two verbs for 'play'. Jogar is for games and sports (jogo tênis), while tocar is for musical instruments (toco piano).
grammar.commonMistake: Using jogar for an instrument. Music takes tocar.

dialogue.setup: Marcos and Sara talk about their weekend plans.

lines.speaker: Marcos
lines.native: O que você gosta de fazer no tempo livre?
lines.english: What do you like to do in your free time?

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.native: Gosto de ler e ir ao cinema.
lines.english: I like reading and going to the cinema.

lines.speaker: Marcos
lines.native: Eu também! Vamos ao cinema hoje?
lines.english: Me too! Shall we go to the cinema today?

lines.speaker: Marcos
lines.native: Uma comédia brasileira. O que você acha?
lines.english: A Brazilian comedy. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Marcos
lines.native: Então a gente se encontra às oito.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I like reading' in Portuguese?
quiz.explanation: Gostar needs de before the verb ler.

quiz.question: Which is correct for 'I like music'?
Jogo _____. (football)
quiz.explanation: Jogar is used for sports like futebol.

quiz.question: Which verb means 'to play' an instrument?
quiz.explanation: Tocar is for instruments; jogar is for games.

quiz.question: Arrange: cinema / ao / vamos
quiz.explanation: Vamos ao cinema? means 'Shall we go to the cinema?'
